title: DryRunLocalSpec
description:
Input spec for the local pre-flight execution gate.

``executor`` is the REAL per-task command (the same string the
cluster dispatcher resolves from the run sidecar — e.g.
``python train.py --seed $SEED``), NOT the job-script's dispatcher
command. It is only consulted when ``smoke`` is set; the default-on
template-render layer needs ``result_dir_template`` + ``tasks.py``
alone.

result_dir_template.description: The per-run result-directory template the cluster dispatcher renders with str.format over {task_id}/{run_id}/<kwargs>. Checked for unfilled placeholder fields and cross-id collisions.
result_dir_template.title: Result Dir Template

tasks_py_path.description: Path to the campaign's tasks.py (relative to experiment_dir).
tasks_py_path.title: Tasks Py Path

run_id.description: run_id fed into the template render + the smoke run's HPC_RUN_ID. A placeholder is fine — the gate never touches the journal; it only needs a value so a template referencing {run_id} resolves.
run_id.title: Run Id

sample_n_tasks.description: Number of task ids to sample from tasks.resolve(i) for the template-render / collision check. Sampling (not an exhaustive walk) keeps the gate fast for large grids; collisions are still caught across the sampled window, which is where the bug class lives.
sample_n_tasks.title: Sample N Tasks

smoke.description: Opt in to actually executing the executor locally for ONE sampled grid point (default OFF). Runs `executor` (or `smoke_command` when given) under a shell with the same HPC_KW_*/bare-upper env the cluster dispatcher exports, to catch import errors + arg-binding bugs before any SSH. Scoped to 'broken code, not broken cluster'.
smoke.title: Smoke

executor.description: The real per-task command (e.g. `python train.py --seed $SEED`). Required when smoke=true. Must not be the dispatcher command itself (the #162 self-recursion footgun) — the gate refuses it.
executor.title: Executor

smoke_command.description: Override the smoke-exec command with an import/--help-level probe the executor opts into (e.g. `python -c 'import train'` or `python train.py --help`). When omitted, the gate runs `executor` verbatim — appropriate only for executors that no-op cheaply.
smoke_command.title: Smoke Command

smoke_task_id.description: Which sampled task id supplies the kwargs/env for the smoke run.
smoke_task_id.title: Smoke Task Id

smoke_timeout_sec.description: Hard wall-clock cap on the local smoke run. A local probe that outlives this is killed and reported as smoke_timeout — the gate is meant to be fast (import/--help), not a real training run.
smoke_timeout_sec.title: Smoke Timeout Sec
